_readme: Edit portfolio facts in this file. Keep valid JSON. Project folders map to images/project/<folder>, and work folders map to images/work/<folder>.

meta.title: Proud — Product Owner · PM · UX/UI · Full-stack · QA
meta.description: Portfolio website content for Ratchaprapa Chattrakulrak.

hero.name: Ratchaprapa C.
hero.description: Product Owner with software, design, business, and QA range. I turn ambiguous problems into clear requirements, aligned priorities, usable flows, and release-ready products.

about.heading: Product Owner who understands the whole product system
paragraphs: My main direction is Product Owner. I like being the person who connects user needs, business goals, technical constraints, and team execution into one clear product direction.

Because I studied Software and Knowledge Engineering, I understand how software is planned, designed, built, tested, and shipped. I can read technical tradeoffs, communicate with developers, and write requirements that are realistic enough for a team to execute.

I also have a strong design sense. In team projects, I was often trusted to lead feature direction and UX/UI because I care about details, user context, and whether the product truly solves a real problem. Design thinking helps me translate messy needs into flows people can actually use.

On the business side, I learned early through selling, freelancing, and running Hokori Studio. I handled client conversations, requirement analysis, costing, accounting, task assignment, and 100+ mostly B2B deals, which shaped how I think about value, market fit, and product decisions.

Around the Product Owner role, I can also support as a Project Manager, UX/UI Designer, Full-stack Developer, and Manual QA. Those skills make me more useful in cross-functional teams because I can understand each side deeply and help move work from idea to release.

qa.question: Why Product Owner?
qa.answer: Because I care most about turning real problems into product decisions. Software gave me the technical base, but PO is where my interests in users, business, design, and delivery meet.

qa.question: Why should a team choose me?
qa.answer: I take ownership early: find pain points, clarify requirements, write user stories and acceptance criteria, align design and engineering, and check the release against the real user problem.

qa.question: What evidence shows I can do PO work?
qa.answer: ByteArk gave me discovery-to-release practice. KU Eater, Courtly, and Referite show product direction, MVP scoping, sprint planning, backlog ownership, and cross-team Agile work.

qa.question: What makes my perspective different?
qa.answer: I combine software fundamentals, UX/UI sense, QA detail, and 100+ client-facing Hokori Studio deals, so I can balance practicality, usability, and business value.

qa.question: How do I handle messy requirements?
qa.answer: I turn ambiguity into structure: research, define the real problem, shape MVP scope, prioritize by impact and feasibility, then keep the team aligned with clear specs.

qa.question: How do I work with engineers and designers?
qa.answer: I translate user and business needs into clear flows, specs, and acceptance criteria, then stay close to design and engineering to catch gaps between intent, feasibility, and implementation early.

qa.question: How do I prioritize product decisions?
qa.answer: I balance user impact, business value, technical feasibility, team capacity, and release risk. When scope is tight, I protect the core value first and cut or defer what does not serve the main flow.

qa.question: What kind of team fits me best?
qa.answer: I fit product-led teams that care about users, move fast, improve continuously, and expect a PO to think deeply, communicate clearly, and take real ownership of outcomes.

qa.question: What are my strengths and growth area?
qa.answer: Strengths: ownership, curiosity, empathy, communication, and detail. Growth area: I can overthink quality, so I manage it with prioritization, timeboxing, acceptance criteria, and tradeoff discussions.

roleCards.title: Product Ownership
roleCards.description: Requirements, user stories, acceptance criteria, backlog clarity, feature prioritisation, and release readiness.

roleCards.title: Project Management
roleCards.description: Sprint planning, stakeholder alignment, scope clarity, progress tracking, team coordination, and delivery follow-through.

roleCards.title: Hands-on Product Skills
roleCards.description: UX/UI design, full-stack development fundamentals, manual QA, and practical collaboration with design and engineering.

experiences.title: Product Owner / PM Intern
bullets: Researched users, competitors, pain points, and personas to shape product direction.
Wrote user stories, RFCs, and acceptance criteria; aligned UI flows with designers and engineers.
Tracked sprint progress, tested releases, and surfaced issues before they became blockers.

experiences.title: Founder — 3D Design Services
bullets: Founded a 3D design service and delivered 100+ interior/exterior client projects.
Owned marketing, scoping, client communication, production, and team coordination.

experiences.title: Core Team Member
bullets: Organised tech workshops, career sessions, and student community events.
Designed Goodoo UI components used as workshop teaching material.

experiences.title: Vice-President & Financial Manager
bullets: Led event teams across BarCamp Bangkhen, JobFair, SOS Camp, and Byenior.
Managed budgets, approvals, procurement documents, and financial records.

experiences.title: Freelancer — Development, Design, AI Media
bullets: Delivered client work across development, graphic design, and AI-generated media.
Learned new tools quickly and adapted to varied client needs.

projectsSection.heading: Selected work
projectsSection.subheading: Product-led projects where I shaped requirements, user flows, team direction, and hands-on execution.

projects.name: SleepSense
projects.description: IoT sleep monitoring system with sensors, MQTT pipeline, ML score, and Streamlit dashboard.

modal.about: SleepSense turns bedroom environment data into sleep-quality insights through IoT sensors, backend processing, ML, and a dashboard.
modal.experience: I worked across product planning, architecture, API flow, data handling, and presentation, translating a messy real-world problem into measurable insights.
challenges: Keeping data consistent from sensors to dashboard.
Making ML results understandable for users.
Balancing PM decisions with hands-on debugging.

projects.name: KU Eater
projects.description: Food recommendation platform for KU cafeterias with reviews, menu browsing, web, and Android flows.

modal.about: KU Eater helps Kasetsart students decide what to eat through recommendations, peer reviews, and menu browsing.
modal.experience: I shaped research, user flows, Figma direction, backlog thinking, and frontend/mobile decisions while keeping the user problem visible.
challenges: Turning broad meal-choice pain points into focused features.
Keeping web and Android flows consistent.
Prioritising features around the core decision journey.

projects.name: Courtly
projects.description: Badminton booking and court-management system for players and real operator workflows.

modal.about: Courtly supports badminton reservations, manager scheduling, check-ins, and operational visibility.
modal.experience: I owned Product Owner work end to end while contributing to frontend, API design, and QA under deadline pressure.
challenges: Cutting scope without weakening the booking flow.
Writing build-ready stories and acceptance criteria.
Switching between leadership and implementation.

projects.name: FurStay
projects.description: Pet-sitting platform with owner, sitter, and admin roles designed around trust and booking clarity.

modal.about: FurStay separates pet owner, sitter, and admin journeys for a clearer pet-sitting service experience.
modal.experience: I helped define product structure, role boundaries, and technical choices across Next.js, Prisma, NextAuth, and PostgreSQL.
challenges: Keeping role permissions and journeys easy to understand.
Designing data around booking, profiles, and admin review.
Balancing marketplace UX with service operations.

projects.name: EcoWear
projects.description: Sustainable fashion UX project covering research, personas, journey maps, wireframes, and Figma UI.

modal.about: EcoWear explores how sustainable shopping can feel clear, rewarding, and easy to act on.
modal.experience: I moved from research to personas, journey maps, wireframes, high-fidelity Figma, and a reusable component direction.
challenges: Showing sustainability signals without slowing shopping.
Turning research insights into interface patterns.
Keeping the visual system consistent.

projects.name: Referite
projects.description: Referee and scoring system for a simulated Paris 2024 suite, managed with Agile/Jira workflows.

modal.about: Referite combines score entry, match management, and referee workflows for a simulated Olympics software suite.
modal.experience: I acted as Product Owner, using Jira for planning, backlog grooming, reviews, and retrospectives.
challenges: Turning Agile theory into real team artifacts.
Reducing ambiguity with acceptance criteria.
Managing sprint scope as deadlines tightened.

projects.name: Planty
projects.description: Prolog knowledge base that recommends plants from environment and care preferences.

modal.about: Planty uses Prolog rules to match users with plants based on care preferences and environment.
modal.experience: This project taught me to model a domain as facts, rules, and queries instead of procedural logic.
challenges: Thinking declaratively in Prolog.
Turning flexible preferences into strict rules.
Seeing where rule-based AI is transparent or brittle.

projects.name: Smoogenie
projects.description: OWL/RDF smoothie recommendation ontology using ingredients, nutrients, diets, and SPARQL queries.

modal.about: Smoogenie models smoothie ingredients, nutrients, flavours, and diets as a semantic recommendation system.
modal.experience: I learned how recommendations can come from structured relationships, not only app logic.
challenges: Designing an ontology that stayed queryable.
Choosing classes and properties carefully.
Making knowledge engineering feel approachable.

projects.name: Goodoo
projects.description: Task-management app design and reusable UI system created for GDSC workshop teaching.

modal.about: Goodoo is a task-management UI designed as clear, implementable workshop material.
modal.experience: I focused on teachable components, clean states, and handoff specs that helped students build faster.
challenges: Designing for beginner developers.
Keeping components simple and reusable.
Writing handoff details that reduced confusion.

projects.name: PowerBank Lender
projects.description: Three-day smart powerbank cabinet prototype with employee login, borrow/return, and alarm concept.

modal.about: PowerBank Lender is a smart cabinet prototype for tracked powerbank borrowing and returning.
modal.experience: A short sprint forced fast prioritisation across UI, hardware behaviour, backend assumptions, and demo flow.
challenges: Delivering a convincing prototype in three days.
Handling borrow, return, and unauthorised removal cases.
Keeping the demo flow understandable.

projects.name: Book Collection
projects.context: Individual Project — first serious coding project
projects.description: Solo Python Tkinter book manager connected to Google Sheets API for real data persistence.

modal.about: Book Collection is a personal book manager where my first serious API integration clicked.
modal.experience: I built the GUI, data flow, and Google Sheets connection myself, using books as a motivating domain.
challenges: Moving from scripts into a structured app.
Learning API credentials, requests, and updates.
Making a personal tool feel usable.

contact.heading: Let's connect
contact.subheading: Open to Product Owner roles, with supporting PM, UX/UI, full-stack, and QA capability.
contact.availability:
Bangkok, Thailand

footer.education: B.Eng. Software & Knowledge Engineering, Kasetsart University
